summary: DHH briefly reviews the MovableType 2.0 release, noting its interface overhaul and multiple categories support. While acknowledging it as a nice package, he finds it underwhelming for his needs, particularly around klogging and notifications. He criticizes the static page generation approach and calls for database support. The post ends with him expressing a desire to move away from Greymatter, another early blogging tool.
key_points: MovableType 2.0 launched with a redesigned interface and multiple category support
Static page regeneration on every design change is a scaling pain point
Database-backed blogging is the clear next step over file-based systems
DHH was already dissatisfied with existing blogging tools, foreshadowing his later work
money_quotes: Building an ever increasing number of pages on the simplest design change is also growing painful. Database support next, please?
I really need to say goodbye to Greymatter soon.
key_insight: Even in 2002, DHH was frustrated by the limitations of static-file blogging tools and pushing for database-backed architectures — a mindset that would soon lead him to build Rails.
